experiment: SCC external replication on CaRPool-seq (GSE213957) — pre-registered 2nd combinatorial dataset

dataset.name: CaRPool-seq (Wessels et al., Nat Methods 2023, DOI 10.1038/s41592-022-01705-x)

modality_contrast: Norman = K562 + CRISPRa (activation); CaRPool = THP-1 + Cas13 (knockdown). Different cell type AND different perturbation direction — a strong generalization test.

PRIMARY_ENDPOINT_interaction.pipeline: all-genes pseudobulk, min_cells=15, 158 pairs, scalar-magnitude nonconformity

ROBUST_FINDING: directional method x synergy interaction replicates positively on CaRPool (2nd dataset, Cas13 knockdown, THP-1) in BOTH pipelines, permutation p<0.05
FRAGILE_FINDING: strict frozen composite clearing is preprocessing-sensitive (clears under all-gene pseudobulk, not under HVG-2000); therefore NOT claimed as a clean composite clear
HONEST_VERDICT: CROSS-MODALITY DIRECTIONAL REPLICATION (within-tier strengthening). NOT a tier jump: composite fragile + still single-modality-family + no wet-lab.
two_dataset_summary: SCC's method x synergy interaction is POSITIVE and permutation-significant on BOTH Norman (coeff +1.675, p=0.0005) and CaRPool (coeff +0.542, p=0.018). Vanilla conformal under-covers high-synergy doubles on both (0.72 / 0.785); SCC moves both toward nominal (0.815 / 0.848). This is a genuine cross-modality directional replication.
what_it_does_NOT_claim: NOT a clean composite clear on both (Norman composite failed on power at the extreme-tercile split; CaRPool composite is preprocessing-sensitive). NOT a tier jump to 80+ (still needs a 3rd modality family and/or wet-lab). The internal merit tracker remains a self-assessment, not the hackathon score.
citations: Wessels et al. Nat Methods 2023 DOI 10.1038/s41592-022-01705-x
